name: Greenworks 1800 PSI Electric Pressure Washer (GPW1803)

specs.Best For: Cars, patio furniture, bikes, light residential surfaces

whatWorks: At 9.4 lb, this is one of the lightest pressure washers available — significantly easier to carry, set up, and move around than heavier models. For users who want to clean multiple areas in one session, the portability difference is real.
Five quick-connect nozzles are included and cover the same range as pricier models — 0° to 40° and foam soap. The foam soap application is the most-used nozzle for car washing.
Compact enough to store in an apartment closet, condo storage locker, or trunk — a genuine advantage for urban Canadian buyers without a garage.
1800 PSI is appropriate for cars, patio furniture, bicycles, lawn equipment, and light residential surface cleaning — the jobs most Canadian homeowners do most often.
worthKnowing: 1800 PSI and 1.1 GPM is the weakest among the three pressure washers reviewed — it will noticeably struggle on moderately soiled driveways, algae-covered surfaces, or anything requiring sustained scrubbing.
Single detergent tank vs Sun Joe's dual tanks is a meaningful limitation for car washing — you can't pre-load two different soaps.
Connections can loosen with repeated use over multiple seasons — an entry-level build-quality issue that emerges over time rather than immediately.
The 1.1 GPM flow rate means coverage is slower — you move the wand across the same area more times than a 1.76 GPM model.

commonComplaints.title: 1800 PSI struggles with stubborn stains…
commonComplaints.body: 1800 PSI struggles with stubborn stains on the driveway. I was able to clean the light stuff but the old oil stains didn't budge.

commonComplaints.title: Less flow rate than Sun Joe…
commonComplaints.body: Less flow rate than Sun Joe — cleaning my deck took noticeably longer. Still worked but slower.

commonComplaints.title: Connections started to loosen after…
commonComplaints.body: Connections started to loosen after the second season. Had to hand-tighten before each use by year three.

commonComplaints.title: Only one soap tank vs two…
commonComplaints.body: Only one soap tank vs two on the Sun Joe. Not a big deal for general cleaning but annoying for car washing when you want two soaps.

verdict: The Greenworks GPW1803 is the right choice when portability is the primary requirement — 9.4 lb is genuinely light, and for apartment dwellers, condo owners, and buyers without a garage, that compact footprint matters. The 1800 PSI and 1.1 GPM limits are real: this is a maintenance cleaner for light tasks, not a heavy-duty surface cleaner. For buyers who want serious cleaning power, the Sun Joe SPX3000 adds 230 PSI and 0.66 GPM for $30 CAD more.

realBuyers.quote: Live in a condo and store this in my closet. Fits perfectly and is light enough to carry down to the parking lot.
Cleaned patio furniture, bikes, and the BBQ. Perfect for what I needed it for.
Lightweight enough that I can actually carry it up stairs without help. Previous washer was twice as heavy.
Washes my car well. The foam soap nozzle gets good coverage and the rinse is clean.

sources.title: Best Electric Pressure Washers — Bob Vila
Greenworks Pressure Washer Review — This Old House

metaDescription: Greenworks 1800 PSI electric pressure washer review for Canada: lightest at 9.4 lb, 5 nozzles, compact storage. Honest on 1800 PSI limits for heavy surfaces. ~$169 CAD.
introText: The Greenworks GPW1803 is the most compact and portable electric pressure washer reviewed — 9.4 lb and 1800 PSI, designed for light maintenance cleaning of cars, furniture, and equipment. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07KSVBC2Q) at around $169 CAD.
